Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Laura Seidl, Esq. 248-982-2102 of the Law Office of Michael Cohen for Plaintiff.
William Rowell, Esq. 510-267-3000 of Buty & Curliano for Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California.

RULING - A R G U E D. Line 13 (549188) and Line 14 (549189) are called simultaneously. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Defendant The Regents of the University of California's motion for summary adjudication on all causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Annette Elliott is granted as to all five causes of action and Ms. Elliott is granted leave to amend to file a second amended complaint only alleging a single cause of action for negligence based solely on the alleged negligent hiring, training and supervision of the Regents' employees subject to the conditions stated in the order on Ms. Elliott's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint. The Regents presented sufficient evidence to satisfy their burden of production on all five causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint and Ms. Elliott did not present any evidence creating a triable dispute on any of those claims. Based on the undisputed facts, the Regents are not vicariously liable for invasion of privacy, IIED, or negligence because defendant Kien Lam's misconduct was not within the scope of his employment nor was it ratified by the Regents. Videotaping people in a bathroom was not related to Mr. Lam's job duties and his misconduct was not foreseeable in light of his duties. Because the Regents took prompt and appropriate steps to investigate Mr. Lam's misconduct, put Mr. Lam on investigatory leave, and precluded Mr. Lam from returning to work until he resigned, the Regents' conduct shows that they did not ratify Mr. Lam's misconduct. The Regents are not directly liable for invasion of privacy per Civil Code 1708.8 because Mr. Lam confessed that he acted alone in installing the camera. The undisputed facts also show that the inoperable tampon dispenser did not constitute a dangerous condition of property. The Regents are not liable for sexual harassment per Government Code 12940 because Mr. Lam's misconduct did not alter the conditions of Ms. Elliott's employment to create an abusive working environment and the Regents did not ratify Mr. Lam's misconduct. There is no evidence that the Regents had any notice of sexual harassment conduct by Mr. Lam prior to learning of the camera in the bathroom. None of the evidence submitted by Ms. Elliott shows that the Regents had advance knowledge of Mr. Lam having placed a camera in the bathroom or that the Regents acted in any way to ratify Mr. Lam's misconduct. While evidence was presented that the Regents knew or should have known that Mr. Lam had engaged in peculiar, offensive and criminal behavior before the discovery of the camera in the bathroom, none of that evidence would put a reasonable person on notice that Mr. Lam was engaging in conduct that violated the privacy rights of any employee or constituted sexual harassment.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave Of Court To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Laura Seidl, Esq. 248-982-2102 of the Law Office of Michael Cohen for Plaintiff.
William Rowell, Esq. 510-267-3000 of Buty & Curliano for Defendant The Regents Of The University Of California.

RULING - A R G U E D. Line 13 (549188) and Line 14 (549189) are called simultaneously. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave Of Court To File Second Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Annette Elliott's motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted in part subject to the following three conditions: a) Ms. Elliott may file a second amended complaint no later than July 23, 2018 only alleging a single cause of action for negligence based solely on the alleged negligent hiring, training and supervision of the Regents' employees; b) at the same time Ms. Elliott files her second amended complaint she must serve verified responses without objections to deem-served discovery to state all facts that support her sole remaining claim, identify all persons who know any of those facts and what facts they know, identify and produce all documents that refer or relate to those facts; and c) Ms. Elliott must agree that defendant The Regents of the University of California may file a summary judgment/adjudication motion directed to her sole remaining claim on 30 days rather than 75 days' notice. Permitting Ms. Elliott to allege a negligent hiring, training and supervision claim comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any time in a case. Conditioning that permission as set forth herein eliminates any possible legal prejudice to the Regents and allows for the resolution of this case without a further continuance of the trial.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Robert Lynch, Esq. and Laura Seidl, Esq. of the Law Offices of Michael Cohen, T:510.832.6436, for plaintiff/opposing party.

In his tentative ruling, Judge Kahn disclosed that the General Counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, but neither that friendship nor any other matter causes him to be other than fair and impartial in deciding this case.
